responses.text: An early report by MTV Lebanon claimed that Carlos Ghosn fled his Tokyo home with the assistance of a paramilitary group disguised as a musical band.
According to this theory, Ghosn was smuggled out of his residence by hiding inside a large musical instrument case.
However, this assertion was quickly challenged as it lacked proof and was considered a rumor that spread rapidly on social media.
Ghosn's wife, Carole, explicitly denied the narrative, telling Reuters that the reports of a musical escape were 'fiction'.
Subsequent investigative findings and CCTV footage contradicted the idea that a band aided his escape from his home.
Security cameras actually captured Ghosn walking right out of his house on the afternoon of December 29, 2019, before walking to a nearby hotel to meet two men.
The group then boarded a train to Osaka, eventually making their way to a hotel near Kansai international airport.

At that secondary location, the two men were seen leaving with two large containers, implying Ghosn was later concealed in one of those boxes before his flight, rather than leaving his house in an instrument case.
He was subsequently transported onto a private jet bound for Istanbul, and from there continued his journey to Beirut.
